457	func TestReadyAndBlocked(t *testing.T) {
458		s := newTestStore(t)
459	
460		a := &Bead{Title: "First"}
461		b := &Bead{Title: "Second"}
462		require.NoError(t, s.Create(a))
463		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
464		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(b.ID, a.ID))
465	
466		// B is blocked by A
467		ready, err := s.Ready()
468		require.NoError(t, err)
469		assert.Len(t, ready, 1)
470		assert.Equal(t, a.ID, ready[0].ID)
471	
472		blocked, err := s.Blocked()
473		require.NoError(t, err)
474		assert.Len(t, blocked, 1)
475		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, blocked[0].ID)
476	
477		// Close A, B becomes ready
478		require.NoError(t, s.Close(a.ID))
479	
480		ready, err = s.Ready()
481		require.NoError(t, err)
482		assert.Len(t, ready, 1)
483		assert.Equal(t, b.ID, ready[0].ID)
484	
485		blocked, err = s.Blocked()
486		require.NoError(t, err)
487		assert.Len(t, blocked, 0)
488	}
489	
490	func TestDepAddValidation(t *testing.T) {
491		s := newTestStore(t)
492	
493		a := &Bead{Title: "A"}
494		require.NoError(t, s.Create(a))
495	
496		// Dep on nonexistent
497		err := s.DepAdd(a.ID, "nonexistent")
498		assert.Error(t, err)
499		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "dependency not found")
500	
501		// Self-dep
502		err = s.DepAdd(a.ID, a.ID)
503		assert.Error(t, err)
504		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot depend on self")
505	
506		// Idempotent add
507		b := &Bead{Title: "B"}
508		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
509		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(b.ID, a.ID))
510		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(b.ID, a.ID)) // no error on duplicate
511	}
512	
513	func TestDepRemove(t *testing.T) {
514		s := newTestStore(t)
515	
516		a := &Bead{Title: "A"}
517		b := &Bead{Title: "B"}
518		require.NoError(t, s.Create(a))
519		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
520		require.NoError(t, s.DepAdd(b.ID, a.ID))
521	
522		got, _ := s.Get(b.ID)
523		assert.Contains(t, got.DepIDs(), a.ID)
524	
525		require.NoError(t, s.DepRemove(b.ID, a.ID))
526		got, _ = s.Get(b.ID)
527		assert.NotContains(t, got.DepIDs(), a.ID)
528	}

15	func TestClaimRecordsMetadata(t *testing.T) {
16		s := newTestStore(t)
17		b := &Bead{Title: "Claimable"}

19	
20		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "agent-1"))
21	
22		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
23		require.NoError(t, err)

25		assert.Equal(t, "agent-1", got.Owner)

30	func TestUnclaimClearsMetadata(t *testing.T) {
31		s := newTestStore(t)
32		b := &Bead{Title: "To unclaim"}
33		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
34		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "agent-1"))

36		require.NoError(t, s.Unclaim(b.ID))
37	
38		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
39		require.NoError(t, err)
40		assert.Equal(t, StatusOpen, got.Status)

641	// withHeartbeat temporarily overrides HeartbeatInterval and HeartbeatTTL for
642	// the duration of a test.
643	func withHeartbeat(t *testing.T, interval, ttl time.Duration) {
644		t.Helper()
645		origInterval := HeartbeatInterval
646		origTTL := HeartbeatTTL
647		HeartbeatInterval = interval
648		HeartbeatTTL = ttl
649		t.Cleanup(func() {
650			HeartbeatInterval = origInterval
651			HeartbeatTTL = origTTL
652		})
653	}
654	
655	func TestHeartbeatReclaimStaleInProgressBead(t *testing.T) {
656		withHeartbeat(t, 10*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)
657	
658		s := newTestStore(t)
659		b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale", Title: "Stale claim"}
660		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
661	
662		// First worker claims it normally.
663		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-a"))
664	
665		// Forge a stale runtime lease and a stale legacy tracker heartbeat.
666		stale := time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour)
667		staleRec := claimLivenessRecord{
668			BeadID:    b.ID,
669			UpdatedAt: stale,
670			PID:       os.Getpid(),
671		}
672		data, err := json.Marshal(staleRec)
673		require.NoError(t, err)
674		require.NoError(t, writeAtomicClaimFile(claimLivenessPath(s.Dir, b.ID), append(data, '\n')))
675		require.NoError(t, s.Update(b.ID, func(bd *Bead) {
676			if bd.Extra == nil {
677				bd.Extra = map[string]any{}
678			}
679			staleStr := stale.Format(time.RFC3339)
680			bd.Extra["execute-loop-heartbeat-at"] = staleStr
681			bd.Extra["claimed-at"] = staleStr
682		}))
683	
684		// A fresh worker must be able to reclaim the stalled bead.
685		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-b"))
686		got, err := s.Get(b.ID)
687		require.NoError(t, err)
688		assert.Equal(t, StatusInProgress, got.Status)
689		assert.Equal(t, "worker-b", got.Owner)
690	
691		// The ready-execution queue must surface the stale bead too.
692		s2 := newTestStore(t)
693		orig := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-stale-2", Title: "Stale claim 2"}
694		require.NoError(t, s2.Create(orig))
695		require.NoError(t, s2.Claim(orig.ID, "worker-a"))
696		staleLease := claimLivenessRecord{
697			BeadID:    orig.ID,
698			UpdatedAt: time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour),
699			PID:       os.Getpid(),
700		}
701		data, err = json.Marshal(staleLease)
702		require.NoError(t, err)
703		require.NoError(t, writeAtomicClaimFile(claimLivenessPath(s2.Dir, orig.ID), append(data, '\n')))
704		ready, err := s2.ReadyExecution()
705		require.NoError(t, err)
706		require.Len(t, ready, 1)
707		assert.Equal(t, orig.ID, ready[0].ID)
708	}
709	
710	func TestHeartbeatKeepsActiveClaimAlive(t *testing.T) {
711		withHeartbeat(t, 5*time.Millisecond, 50*time.Millisecond)
712	
713		s := newTestStore(t)
714		b := &Bead{ID: "ddx-hb-live", Title: "Live claim"}
715		require.NoError(t, s.Create(b))
716	
717		require.NoError(t, s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-a"))
718	
719		// Actively refresh the heartbeat for longer than the TTL.
720		deadline := time.Now().Add(150 * time.Millisecond)
721		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
722			require.NoError(t, s.TouchClaimHeartbeat(b.ID))
723			time.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)
724		}
725	
726		// The last heartbeat was just written — well within the TTL window.
727		// Worker B must NOT reclaim an actively-heartbeating bead.
728		err := s.Claim(b.ID, "worker-b")
729		require.Error(t, err)
730		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "cannot claim")
731	
732		// It must also not appear in the ready-execution queue.
733		ready, err := s.ReadyExecution()
734		require.NoError(t, err)

data.text: All pre-commit checks pass. Now commit:

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(bead/store): unify Ready definition - drop redundant Status==Open filter

The readyFiltered helper applied a second Status==StatusOpen guard in the
operator-facing path, causing stale-claimed in_progress beads to be invisible
to `ddx bead ready` even though the lifecycle classifier had already promoted
them to LifecycleBucketReady. Remove the guard; both executionOnly and
operator-facing paths now trust the classifier's bucket decision directly.

Adds TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady to cover the regression. [ddx-c113a116]
EOF
)"

data.text:
Commit landed. All ACs are satisfied:

1. **AC1**: `readyFiltered` in `store.go` no longer applies a `Status==StatusOpen` filter — both paths return entries with `Bucket==LifecycleBucketReady`.
2. **AC2**: `TestReady_StaleClaimedInProgressIsReady` in `port_gaps_test.go` asserts a stale-claimed in_progress bead appears in `Ready()`.
3. **AC3**: All existing bead tests remain green.
4. **AC4**: `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/...` — bead and cmd green; the one agent failure (`TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle`) is a pre-existing flaky test that fails on the base with `-count=5`.
5. **AC5**: `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
